concerns.text: T026 mandates halting the analysis pipeline if the manipulation check fails (mean > 4.0). This violates FR-003 and US-2 which require the system to execute planned contrasts and report results (including null findings) regardless of the outcome. The task introduces a silent constraint relaxation by blocking execution rather than flagging the dataset, effectively weakening the requirement to 'execute' the analysis.

T015 defines the Lee & See (2004) Trust Scale using exactly five specific items. SC-004 and FR-002 require capturing trust data using the 'exact item set' defined in the source instrument. The standard Lee & See (2004) scale typically includes 12+ items across multiple dimensions. By hardcoding only 5 items without explicit spec authorization for a shortened version, this task silently relaxes the fidelity requirement of SC-004, risking invalid verification against the primary source.

T029 schedules the 'pre-study power analysis' implementation in Phase 5 (US3). The Spec (SC-002) and Plan (Phase 0) require this calculation to be performed *before* data collection to determine sample size. Implementing this logic in Phase 5 creates a semantic gap where the system cannot satisfy the 'pre-study' constraint during the actual data collection phase, effectively weakening the temporal requirement of the success criterion.
